Hey, what's going on? Okay, so let's get right to it. These sales FAQ. Okay, someone says, hey, what's the membership price? This is something that you don't want to do with this in the initial consultation because you don't want to sell two things at once. You want to just sell this sale and then sell them on how great you are while you're fulfilling. So what's the price? Be like, don't worry about the price. It's less than the challenge. It won't get you a great deal. Let's just get you started. Let's just give you great results first and then we can talk about it. Sound good? Great. All of these, it's not a hard obstacle to overcome. You just say, listen, it depends on how long you're staying, but I can promise you it's cheaper than the challenge, so don't worry about it. We'll give you a great deal. What time do you want to come in? What I just said. Just sell them on the actual sale that you're focused on. Tell them that it's going to be cheaper than the challenge going forward, but don't worry about that right now. Let's just get you great results. Sound good? Great. Can I try a session first? I don't really get these a lot. Some people do, but it's really, really rare. It means that you haven't really sold them on the end result because they shouldn't even be thinking about workouts or meal plans or anything because they should be focusing on the end goal or the vacation of where they're trying to go. What it will be is going to be exactly what you need to get the results you're looking for. Then that's it. It shouldn't be focused on, well, I like the sessions. It should be focused on what result am I going to be getting because that's the whole point. How much should my sales piece just say, awesome, you want one of the last box? They say yes, you're like, awesome, you have your ID on you? Then they pull their ID. When they pull their ID out, they pull their wallet out. Then they give you your ID and you can see that they have credit cards in there. Then after you write all the info that they have from their ID, then you say, hey, let me trade you. What card do you want to use? You can point at their wallet. What card do you want to use? Then they'll swap it with you because now they can't say they forgot their wallet at home or whatever. They're like, well, I don't have the card that I want to use, but they'll put this one on and you can call me to switch it out tomorrow. We'll put the first billing for tomorrow in that way. You can call me beforehand and swap it out otherwise it'll run in this card, but that way you won't forget. That way I can reserve the spot for you. Works fine. The money trick with the ID is a great way to get them to take their wallet out. It's like an escalation. You want to do it? Yes. Okay, cool. Do you have your ID on you? Cool. That's like an intermediate. Okay, cool. I'll come up and it works well. How should I sell this to existing members? I covered this in the lead gen section, but you should be meeting with them in person just like you would a normal sales system. Say, hey, I'm kind of interested in that. Awesome. Can you come in tomorrow at whatever and just put them in the normal reminder sequence that you normally would do? What should I charge for my challenge? $4.99 is what I recommend doing. All the guys who've been with me in Legacy are almost all of them are $4.99 or $5.99. Some people start because they don't believe that they can sell it, but I promise you that the limiting beliefs are only in your mind, not in the mind of your prospects. All right, we sold it at $5.99 in the absolute ghetto. You'll be fine. What's the bet for bulking? 15 pounds of actual weight gain in six weeks is a good bet for bulking. I've done it for women and men. For women, I might do 10 pounds. For men, I'll do 15 or 20, depending on the size of the guy. I don't really have a rhyme or reason for it. We just set that goal based on where they're at. If a guy's 130 pounds, probably not going to gain 20, so I'll make it 15. If a guy's $1.90, then I'll make it 2.10. It's his goal. That's a percentage thing. I wish I had more objective numbers to give you, but that's just 15 to go to 220. It's an easy way to do it. How do I get less partial payments? You decide to get less partial payments. You just start saying you have to pay half before you start, or you can say you have to pay everything before you start and you just push out their start date. Either those work for me, what I did, I think the happy balance is just saying you have to have half down and then you have to be paid out by week two. That's pretty much what I did with my whole sales team. Didn't decrease our sales whatsoever. I have all of this point, but they're all filled out. I'd stack those on top of the fresh printouts and I'd only have three or four printouts that were blank on the table. When I'd say, listen, if we have that many spots left, I'd put my hand on or I'd point towards the stack of contracts and they're like, holy pups. My sales costs are too long. How do I shorten them? You've got to follow the script and stick with it. Obviously, if it's taking too long, it's because you're talking too much about the things, about the program, which is not selling them the vacation. The only thing you're talking about is them. The only time you talk about what we do is usually about a minute and a half. It should be. It's fitness, nutrition, accountability. Fitness-wise, can you work out with us three days a week? You think you can do that? Awesome. Cool. Of those times that work for you? Great. Nutrition-wise, do you think you can follow a plan that looks like this? We've had 12 year olds, people don't speak English, you can read it. It's just color coded. It's as easy as it gets. Do you think you can follow this? Awesome. Great. You can also follow the instruction on the grocery list on the back. This is going to help you with the groceries. Can we over deliver? Great. Awesome. Accountability. Then you go, there's three things. We're going to do your own coach. The coach is going to reach out to you every week just to check in with you, make sure that you're doing okay. This is an amazing program of all time. You don't need to dive really deep on here because then that gives them more things to say no to. Tell me more about how the workouts are structured. Tell me more about how this meal plan works. That's called an analyst frame. They're trying to break your momentum by asking details so that they can find a deeth so they can say no to. When they say meal plans, they're like, listen, you're going to be more protein, you're going to be less carbs, you're going to be less fat. All the stuff you know that you should be doing, you're going to be doing that and you're just going to be doing it consistently because you're going to put money on the line. Work out there. If it's like four or more, then it really becomes a pitch and then you have to start mentally dealing with the obstacle overcomes that would typically come up during the pitch. Rather than asking them questions, you just actually say it. These people have all these questions in their head and you have to check them off for them as you say it. You're trying to talk more and doing less question asking. It's just the name of the game. You're like, hey, who here's done weight lectures? Okay, who here's done Virginia Craig? Is this what happened? You know what I'm talking about. Okay, what about, have you done a gym membership yet? The only time they call you is awesome. So then what's changed? Are you afraid to do it? I understand that. Honestly, I have this conversation all the time. That way it doesn't make them feel an exception. This is totally normal. We talk to people all the time who are scared to start. They're like, I think I'm going to be really busy. You're always going to be busy. You came here and you asked me to hold you accountable to setting the goal. Are you happy where you're at? No. You kind of just have to re-close them on the fact that they want to lose weight. If they still push back, I give them their money back. I don't want that on my conscious and we have a machine that makes so much money, it doesn't really matter. Where does 87% success rate come from? That was the best success rate that we had when we were running the challenge. It varied. I just went off the best success percentage because it was compelling. We still averaged across the board plus 70%, every time, just about every time. You should be fine. You just need to make sure that they're following the plan. As long as you do your job and make sure that they follow the plan, if you don't follow up with them about following the plan, they're not going to follow it. You need to have the conviction that they don't have. I'm going to ask you to do something which is a big ask. You need to trust me. This plan works. We've done it a bajillion times. You just need to tell them to follow it and you need to believe in them. The moment that they have a question about it, they're going to look to you and if you stutter, you hesitate, they're going to take another step forward and say, this doesn't work. You have to come back and be like, you're not following it. I'm just telling you 99 times out of 100, that's the situation. Do you not feel bad about it? Adherence is the reason why a lot of people fail. That's because adherence is hard. What do people get upset about the bait and switch? It's really rare if you're presenting the opportunity the right way. They have to understand why you do it that way. They have to understand the ham and the garlic. They have to understand that we did it in the past without the deposit. We did it the way that they're saying. It just didn't work. How can we still make it effectively free? How can we still make it free to you but still get you the result you're looking for? We put this deposit in place. Everyone's results went through the roof and then we actually served our customers the way that they wanted to be served. That way they won't be upset about it. It makes sense. How is it safe for someone to lose 20 pounds? The reality is that they're not losing 20 pounds of tissue. A lot of times it's like in stores and water. If someone says, is it safe to lose that much weight? It happens all the time. We've been doing it for years. Yes, it's totally safe. Another question is, is it safe to be 250 pounds? Not really. I would ask them, what particular part would you find unsafe? I don't know. I feel like I've heard that. We've also heard that you can't eat carbs. I've heard that you should eat four of this stuff, drink a little bit of water, get a little dehydrated. Just that, what they eat to perform, what they do afterwards usually accounts for eight to 10 pounds. We know that if you're going to lose eight to 10 pounds from a little bit of the gamification, we get that. We built that into the challenge. That's how we know that what you are losing, even if you game it a little bit, you're going to lose 10 real pounds of tissue, 12 real pounds of tissue over these six weeks, which is one to two pounds, which is totally normal. They're like, okay, I get it. That's why. Because if it were a 10 pound challenge, you could just eat a lot tomorrow and then not do anything and then run with a trash bag on the last day and then you'd lose 10 pounds. That make sense? Yeah, okay, cool. Seating arrangements. A lot of people ask me what's the best way to actually sit in the sales consultation. I use those clipboards. I have fresh contracts on them so that I can just hand them to people or I can fill them out and then hand them to them to fill out the credit card stuff or whatever. If it's multiple people coming in, it's one on one. I usually fill out the whole contract for them so I know that it's filled out right. In terms of seating arrangements, I never sit across from a prospect very rarely. I can't even think of the time that I would sit across from a prospect because then it feels like a sale. Normally, I'll just have someone come in and I'll just pull up a chair for them and I'll pull up a chair for me and I'll just sit basically like kitty corner like this. I'll sit like this with them. I'm facing them but I'm not directly facing them but more like this. I can just talk to them. We're sitting buddy to buddy, shoulder to shoulder. That way we feel like we're on the same team and it's not me against you. It's me with you seeing if I can get you to the finish line. I think it's a subconscious thing. Lean on it so it was casual. It was just more of like hey, we're talking about this as presentation rather than is this something whatever. I don't want it to feel like a pitch. Actually one of my best, one of the, okay, how do I politely eject a negative Nancy in a group sale? You say hey, so right after you're like okay. This is really key for any kind of group sales. When you get to the end, you get to the pitch or you presented the offer like okay, who wants one of the last spots or has some more questions before moving forward? That's the question. Then you raise your hand as you say it. Okay. Who wants one of the last spots or has a question or two before moving forward? Then everyone raises their hands because now they all think everyone assumes that everyone else is going to be raising their hands because they're going to be moving forward. They're doing it but some of them might have questions but it looks like an overwhelming percentage of the room is doing it and that way you can get the whole group behind you. If someone doesn't raise your hand, you're like man, totally understand. I don't want to, if what I need to think about it, you should do it now otherwise you're not going to need more spots. What if blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, don't worry we have a money back guarantee on the fact that if we don't provide you excellent service we'll give your money back. I use that all the time and I never had anyone take me up on it. Just a satisfaction guarantee and it worked great. What if someone doesn't have either? What about a percentage of total pounds lost like 10%? That's just not the challenge. If someone comes in at 20% body fat and it's a girl, she can lose 5% body fat. You can get down to 15% especially if someone hasn't worked out in a while. It's really rare that you're going to have someone who's like true great shape who walks in the door and if they do just sell them on a membership. We're selling the challenge that doesn't have the money back for $2.99 if they're just trying to get their butt kicked. It's such a small percentage of people. We would usually sell 2 thirds on the weight loss, 1 third on the body fat percentage and it worked great. I'd recommend doing that. Great, you have to get that in your head. It is free. You will write refund checks to some people and when you write those checks you will understand that those people literally got six weeks of training, nutrition, a support, everything for nothing and they got great results. You have to understand that it's going to happen and it is effectively free for these people so there's nothing wrong with advertising that way. You're fine. Okay, that is it for the sales FAQ for right now and I look forward to the next FAQ.
